title: PPET Exam — Form 2
description: Pediatric Emergency Transport — Form 2. Additional practice questions covering pediatric assessment, airway management, resuscitation, shock, and emergency transport for the PPET certification exam.

questions.text: Which of the following is the MOST reliable early indicator of shock in a pediatric patient?
What is the correct initial IV fluid bolus for a child in hypovolemic shock?
When using a Broselow Tape, you measure the child from head to heel and the tape falls in the red zone. What does this indicate?
options: The child is in the preschool age range
The child's blood pressure is critically low

questions.text: What is the correct compression-to-ventilation ratio for 1-rescuer infant CPR?
A 3-year-old presents with a barking cough, low-grade fever, and inspiratory stridor. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?
Which of the following is an indication of respiratory FAILURE (not just distress) in a child?
A 6-month-old infant presents with fever, bulging fontanelle, and high-pitched cry. What condition should you suspect?
Which site is PREFERRED for intraosseous (IO) access in infants and young children?
A 2-year-old is found unresponsive with no breathing and no pulse. What is the correct initial energy dose for defibrillation?
Which of the following BEST describes the Pediatric Assessment Triangle (PAT)?
options: A bedside tool requiring vital signs and ECG monitoring
A rapid visual assessment of appearance, work of breathing, and circulation to skin
A scoring system based on GCS, heart rate, and blood pressure
A head-to-toe physical examination performed in under 2 minutes

questions.text: A 4-year-old is having a generalized tonic-clonic seizure that has lasted 8 minutes and has not stopped. What is this condition called?
What is the MOST common cause of cardiac arrest in the pediatric population?
options: Respiratory failure or shock leading to asphyxia

questions.text: A child presents with urticaria, wheezing, and hypotension after eating peanuts. What is the FIRST-LINE treatment?
Which of the following findings is MOST consistent with epiglottitis in a child?
options: High fever, drooling, and tripod position
Sudden onset of stridor without fever in a toddler

questions.text: A 10-month-old infant is in cardiopulmonary arrest. Two rescuers are present. What is the PREFERRED chest compression technique?
Normal blood glucose for a pediatric patient is approximately:
When should you suspect non-accidental trauma (child abuse) in a pediatric patient?
options: Any fracture in a child under age 10
Injury pattern inconsistent with the child's developmental age or history
Any bruising in a mobile child
Only when the child reports abuse directly

questions.text: What is the minimum systolic blood pressure considered acceptable for a 5-year-old child?
A 3-month-old infant is found unresponsive and not breathing. After activating EMS, what is the NEXT step?
options: Begin 30 compressions then check for pulse
Open the airway and give 2 rescue breaths

questions.text: Bronchiolitis is MOST commonly caused by which pathogen?
Which of the following BEST describes grunting in an infant?
options: Auto-PEEP to maintain alveolar patency — a critical sign of respiratory distress
A normal finding in neonates under 1 week

questions.text: What is the correct depth of chest compression for a child (1–8 years)?
A 7-year-old is in respiratory distress with diffuse expiratory wheezing. His home albuterol MDI has not helped. What is the NEXT step?
options: Administer nebulized albuterol and consider magnesium sulfate

questions.text: Which of the following is a feature of the APPEARANCE component of the Pediatric Assessment Triangle?
An 18-month-old is brought in with sudden onset of coughing and choking while eating. She is now quiet with decreased breath sounds on the right and mild stridor. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?
Which of the following is TRUE about febrile seizures?
options: They are most common in children aged 6 months to 5 years
They cause permanent brain damage in most cases
They require anticonvulsant therapy after the first episode

questions.text: When suctioning a pediatric patient's airway, how long should each suction attempt last?
A 6-year-old involved in a motor vehicle collision is awake and crying. His pelvis is unstable on compression. Which type of shock is MOST likely?
Which of the following is the MOST appropriate BVM mask size for a term newborn?
options: Any mask that creates a seal

questions.text: A 2-year-old has a capillary refill time of 4 seconds. This finding is MOST consistent with:
options: Normal perfusion in a cold environment

questions.text: In pediatric trauma, what anatomical feature makes children MORE prone to solid organ (liver/spleen) injury than adults?
options: Thinner, more pliable rib cage with less protection
Larger kidneys relative to body size

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes the normal respiratory rate for a 1-year-old infant?
A 9-year-old is found unresponsive after a near-drowning. He has no pulse. After beginning CPR, the AED is attached and advises 'no shock recommended.' What is the MOST likely rhythm?
What is the MOST common cause of pulseless electrical activity (PEA) in pediatric patients?
When performing bag-mask ventilation on a child, what is the MOST important indicator that ventilation is adequate?
options: The bag is being squeezed completely with each breath
Visible chest rise with each breath

questions.text: A 5-year-old presents with sudden onset of high fever, a petechial rash, and altered mental status. What condition should you IMMEDIATELY suspect?
A 4-year-old has a suspected forearm fracture after a fall. The limb is deformed but there is no open wound. What is the APPROPRIATE prehospital management?
options: Straighten the arm and apply a rigid splint
Splint in position found with a padded board splint, assess neurovascular status before and after
Transport without splinting to avoid causing further injury

questions.text: How is the estimated weight (in kg) of a child calculated using the pediatric formula for ages 1–10 years?
When should an AED be used on an infant under 1 year of age?
options: Never — AED use is contraindicated under age 1
Only if a manual defibrillator is not available
It is the preferred method at any age
Only with pediatric pads and attenuator if available; manual defibrillator is preferred

questions.text: Which of the following is TRUE regarding nasopharyngeal airways (NPAs) in pediatric patients?
options: They can be used in conscious patients with an intact gag reflex
They are preferred over OPAs in all pediatric patients
They are contraindicated in any child under age 5
They require the child to be deeply unconscious for placement

questions.text: A school-age child with known diabetes is found confused and diaphoretic. Blood glucose reads 42 mg/dL. What is the APPROPRIATE treatment?
options: Give oral glucose if the patient can swallow, or IV dextrose if not
Give 20 mL/kg NS bolus for hypoperfusion
Place in recovery position and transport immediately without treatment

questions.text: Which of the following is a hallmark sign of distributive (septic) shock in a child?
options: Cool, pale, mottled skin with delayed cap refill
Warm, flushed skin with bounding pulses despite hypotension

questions.text: A 12-year-old athlete collapses during a sports event and is found in ventricular fibrillation. What energy setting should be used for the SECOND defibrillation attempt?
Which of the following is an early sign of increased intracranial pressure (ICP) in a child?
A 3-year-old is in respiratory arrest. After opening the airway and finding no breathing, how many rescue breaths should you give before beginning CPR (1-rescuer scenario)?
A 7-year-old has burns to both arms and the anterior trunk. Using the Rule of Nines (modified for pediatrics), approximately what percentage of BSA is burned?
Which of the following findings is MOST consistent with cardiogenic shock in a child?
options: Flat neck veins and cool, clammy skin
JVD, crackles in the lungs, and cool extremities
Warm, flushed skin with widened pulse pressure

questions.text: What is the PREFERRED method to confirm endotracheal tube placement in a pediatric patient during cardiac arrest?
options: Auscultation of bilateral breath sounds only
Visualization of the cords during intubation only

questions.text: A 2-month-old presents with poor feeding, weak cry, and constipation for 5 days. The infant has poor muscle tone and a weak suck. What condition should you suspect?
In a child with a suspected head injury and a GCS of 8, what is the MOST important prehospital intervention?
options: Establish two large-bore IVs and give 40 mL/kg NS
Ensure adequate oxygenation and ventilation while preventing hypotension

questions.text: What is the NORMAL heart rate range for a school-age child (6–12 years)?
Which of the following scenarios MOST warrants activation of a pediatric trauma team rather than a standard transport?
options: A 10-year-old with an isolated closed tibia fracture after a bicycle fall
A 3-year-old restrained passenger in a low-speed MVA with no complaints
An 8-year-old unrestrained passenger in a high-speed rollover with altered mental status
A 6-year-old with a forehead laceration requiring sutures

questions.text: Which structure is responsible for the higher-pitched voice in young children compared to adults?
options: Smaller, narrower subglottic airway (cricoid ring is the narrowest point)

questions.text: A 4-year-old swallowed a button battery 2 hours ago and is now drooling and refusing to eat. What is the MOST appropriate action?
options: Give water to help the battery pass
Transport immediately — button battery in the esophagus is a surgical emergency
Induce vomiting to expel the battery

questions.text: Which of the following oxygen delivery devices provides the HIGHEST concentration of oxygen for a spontaneously breathing child?
A 5-year-old has ingested an unknown quantity of a grandparent's cardiac medication. He is bradycardic and hypotensive. Which medication class is MOST likely responsible?
What is the MOST effective way to reduce anxiety and improve assessment accuracy in a toddler during a pediatric call?
options: Separate the child from the caregiver immediately for a focused exam
Approach quickly and confidently to establish authority
Keep the caregiver involved, use a calm voice, and perform the least threatening assessments first
Restrain the child to prevent movement during assessment
